name: v0.2 adversarial: polite-but-repetitive flat drift escalates toward vent
description: Test vector derived from Grok field report: constant R=2,H=1,O=0 for multiple cycles (low heat, low optionality loss) can still be corrosive drift. This vector is meant to be used with examples/coordcard-v0.2-example.json.

notes: This is an adversarial edge case: low heat / low optionality loss, but repetition hits max (R=3).
In the current TS reference implementation, any component hitting 3 triggers repair/escalation deterministically.
If an implementation wants to tolerate R-only drift longer, encode that as a card-level dial (not hidden heuristics).
The goal of this fixture is parity testing across implementations (TS vs Python), not prescribing a single moral outcome.
